message:
feat(tui): BOCPD resize coalescer + parity matrix tests (1mfw3.2.2, 1mfw3.2.7)

Enable Bayesian Online Changepoint Detection for principled steady/burst
regime switching during terminal resize events. The responsive preset
provides lower thresholds for faster detection, matching cass's
interactive search-as-you-type profile.

Changes:
- Extract build_resize_config() for testable, mode-independent config
- BocpdConfig::responsive() with evidence logging enabled
- EvidenceSink writes to data_dir/resize_evidence.jsonl
- CASS_BOCPD=0 env var disables BOCPD (rate heuristic fallback)
- CASS_RESIZE_EVIDENCE_FILE env var overrides evidence path
- 8 tests: BOCPD defaults, env disable, timing bounds, evidence sink,
  data_dir parity, default enablement, responsive preset, mode matrix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -395,12 +395,12 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","title":"Add palette query latency instrumentation and micro-bench toggle","description":"Objective:
Add low-overhead latency instrumentation for palette query updates and an opt-in micro-bench mode to detect performance regressions.

Deliverables:
- Last-query latency capture.
- Optional benchmark mode with throughput signal (queries/sec).
- Visual budget indicator for query responsiveness.

Rationale:
Making latency visible helps prevent UX regressions as command catalog/features grow.

Done when:
Operators can quickly verify palette responsiveness from within the TUI.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:23.007851279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.597488555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:23.007851279Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:55.597408966Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","title":"Enable BOCPD resize coalescer and surface resize decisions in UI","description":"Workstream .2: Enable BOCPD resize coalescer and surface resize decisions in UI.

Background:
cass currently runs on ftui runtime with budget + mouse enabled, but does not fully wire advanced ProgramConfig knobs for resize evidence and regime-aware coalescing. FrankenTUI includes a resize coalescer with BOCPD support and decision logs.

Goals:
1) Wire ProgramConfig with explicit resize coalescer settings (logging + BOCPD).
2) Capture/summarize resize decisions in cass-facing model state.
3) Surface resize behavior in inspector so users can diagnose resize storms.
4) Keep behavior deterministic and robust across fullscreen/inline/macro paths.

In scope:
- run_tui_ftui config unification and upgrade.
- Evidence plumbing and summary model.
- Inspector presentation of resize regime/action/evidence.
- Test coverage for resize storms and fallback behavior.

Out of scope:
- Full explainability cockpit UX (tracked in .3).

Validation expectations:
- No regressions in macro record/playback paths.
- Resize behavior remains stable under rapid terminal changes.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.384452802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34.870097395Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.384452802Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation guidance: treat runtime config parity as non-negotiable. Any knob added for standard mode must be reviewed for record/playback + inline/fullscreen paths. Evidence wiring should fail soft (graceful empty state) rather than fail closed (broken UI path).","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","title":"Unify ProgramConfig construction across standard, record, and playback paths","description":"Objective:
Refactor run_tui_ftui so all execution modes share one explicit ProgramConfig construction path.

Deliverables:
- Common config builder with mode-specific deltas only where required.
- Consistent runtime knobs across standard, macro recording, and playback paths.
- Reduced configuration drift risk.

Why this matters:
Without a unified builder, advanced runtime options are easy to wire in one path and forget in others, creating invisible behavioral inconsistency.

Done when:
All launch paths consume the same config baseline and differences are intentional and documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:34.183111790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:02:45.478633008Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T01:02:45.478609584Z","close_reason":"Unified: all 3 paths (standard/record/playback) share one ProgramConfig + Program::with_config entry point","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:34.183111790Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","title":"Enable BOCPD + logging CoalescerConfig profile for cass runtime","description":"Objective:
Select and wire an explicit resize coalescer profile with BOCPD enabled and decision logging turned on.

Deliverables:
- CoalescerConfig settings for steady/burst/deadline behavior.
- BOCPD enabled with sane defaults for cass interaction profile.
- Logging configuration suitable for UI-facing evidence summaries.

Why this matters:
Regime-aware resize behavior is core to responsiveness under resize storms; logging is required for explainability and debugging.

Done when:
Runtime is using explicit BOCPD/logging settings in all relevant launch modes.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:47.528311592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T01:07:14.794628288Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:47.528311592Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.009687150Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","title":"Wire resize decision evidence capture path into cass model layer","description":"Objective:
Capture resize decision/evidence signals emitted by runtime and make them available to cass model state.

Deliverables:
- Evidence ingestion hook(s) from runtime output.
- Parsing/normalization into typed cass-side structures.
- Error-tolerant handling of missing/partial evidence events.

Why this matters:
Evidence must exist in model memory before any inspector/cockpit panel can explain adaptive behavior.

Done when:
cass model can access latest resize decision and associated evidence summary during rendering.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:55.716818104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.331809786Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:56:55.716818104Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.331718115Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.4","title":"Define cass resize evidence summary state and rendering-ready schema","description":"Objective:
Design minimal but sufficient cass-side state for resize evidence presentation.

Deliverables:
- Structs for latest action/regime/timing/evidence contributions.
- Ring buffer or snapshot policy for recent resize decisions.
- Backfill/default semantics when evidence is absent.

Why this matters:
A stable local schema decouples UI rendering from raw runtime event shape and supports safe evolution.

Done when:
Inspector/cockpit consumers can read one coherent typed summary object.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.327564236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.690066286Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.327564236Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:59:59.689993640Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.5","title":"Render resize regime and decision summary in inspector","description":"Objective:
Expose resize decision state in the existing inspector path with concise, operator-meaningful fields.

Deliverables:
- Inspector section/tab rows for regime, action, cadence, and key evidence hints.
- Sensible fallback copy when no resize evidence is available yet.
- Non-intrusive layout behavior in smaller terminal sizes.

Why this matters:
Runtime behavior must be understandable in-product for fast diagnosis and trust.

Done when:
Operators can observe resize behavior transitions without external logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.408382454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:00.038373268Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.408382454Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:00.038296675Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.6","title":"Add deterministic resize storm tests and evidence integrity checks","description":"Objective:
Validate resize behavior and evidence integrity with comprehensive unit and e2e coverage under deterministic and storm-like conditions.

Deliverables:
- Unit tests for repeated, oscillating, and burst resize sequences with deterministic expected transitions.
- E2e resize-storm scripts that drive realistic terminal resize patterns and verify applied actions/regimes.
- Detailed structured logging artifacts from e2e runs (decision stream, evidence contributions, regime transitions, timings).
- Failure-focused summary output that pinpoints first divergence from expected decision behavior.

Rationale:
Resize logic is a core responsiveness primitive and must remain stable, explainable, and reproducible.

Done when:
Both unit and e2e resize suites pass with sufficient telemetry to diagnose any future regressions quickly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.863195535Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:06:51.942417584Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.863195535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:01.046780574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:00.650617356Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","title":"Verify macro and inline/fullscreen parity after resize config upgrade","description":"Objective:
Prove runtime parity across macro record/playback and inline/fullscreen modes using scenario-driven e2e validation.

Deliverables:
- Mode matrix e2e scripts (standard, record, playback, inline, fullscreen).
- Detailed logging per scenario (mode, config knobs, observed resize behavior, replay/record lifecycle events, key assertions).
- Regression report capturing intentional vs unintentional behavioral differences.
- Fixes or guardrails for any parity drift.

Rationale:
Configuration divergence across runtime modes is a common source of hidden production bugs.

Done when:
Parity checks pass across supported modes with explicit logs proving equivalent behavior where expected.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.961042056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:06:51.567970664Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.961042056Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:01.504699105Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:01.845945212Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

All launch modes (standard, inline, record, playback) share identical BOCPD/coalescer/evidence config.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:12.961042056Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:01.504699105Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:01.845945212Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","title":"Upgrade inspector to explainability cockpit (diff/resize/budget timeline)","description":"Workstream .3: Upgrade inspector to explainability cockpit (diff/resize/budget timeline).

Background:
Current inspector has useful timing/layout/hit-region tabs but limited causal explanation. FrankenTUI showcase demonstrates explainability cockpit patterns that unify diff strategy, resize regime, and budget/degradation decisions.

Goals:
1) Expand inspector into a practical explainability cockpit.
2) Provide compact timeline context for adaptive runtime decisions.
3) Make performance/debug investigations possible without external log spelunking.

In scope:
- New/expanded inspector tab architecture.
- Panels for diff strategy summary, resize summary, budget/degradation summary.
- Timeline feed correlating major decision events.
- Responsive overlay behavior for constrained terminals.

Out of scope:
- Offline analytics dashboards outside TUI runtime loop.

Validation expectations:
- Cockpit renders safely across degradation levels and terminal sizes.
- Added data never crashes when evidence is absent/partial.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.853555598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:35.627601254Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.853555598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:02.157691206Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Design note: cockpit UX should optimize for diagnosis latency. Prefer compact, high-signal fields and causal timeline snippets over verbose text dumps. The best cockpit answers: what changed, why it changed, and what the system is doing now.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.1","title":"Define explainability cockpit information architecture and data contract","description":"Objective:
Specify cockpit panel structure, data requirements, and fallback behavior before implementation.

Deliverables:
- Panel taxonomy (diff/resize/budget/timeline).
- Required fields and source of truth per panel.
- Empty/error-state policies for missing telemetry.

Why this matters:
A clear IA contract prevents fragmented UI and unbounded scope during implementation.

Done when:
Cockpit implementation can proceed against a stable, explicit panel/data blueprint.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:22.196005382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:22.196005382Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:22.196005382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.2","title":"Implement inspector tab/state expansion for cockpit surfaces","description":"Objective:
Extend inspector/cockpit state model and tab routing so new explainability panels can be rendered predictably.

Deliverables:
- Tab enum/state updates and navigation behavior.
- Overlay sizing and focus behavior updates.
- Backward-safe behavior for existing inspector functionality.

Why this matters:
State architecture must be stable before panel content is added.

Done when:
New cockpit sections can be toggled/rendered without breaking existing inspector UX.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:40.267472549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:02.450022115Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:57:40.267472549Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:02.449917660Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
